title: The Route That Was Never There
summary: A static blackhole route in the TGW route table has silently dropped payroll VPC east-west traffic for six months. Discovered via Reachability Analyzer during new-VPC routing setup.

glossary.Transit Gateway: An AWS network hub that connects VPCs and on-premises networks through a central routing plane. Instead of peering every VPC to every other VPC, each VPC attaches to the TGW and the TGW routes packets between them.
glossary.TGW route table: A routing table associated with a Transit Gateway that determines where packets are forwarded based on destination CIDR. Each attachment is associated with a route table and propagates routes into it.
glossary.Static route: A manually created route in a TGW route table that maps a CIDR to a specific attachment or marks it as a blackhole. Static routes are created by operators and have higher priority than propagated routes for the same CIDR.
glossary.Propagated route: A route that a VPC attachment automatically advertises into a TGW route table when propagation is enabled. Propagated routes reflect the actual CIDR of the attached VPC and are suppressed by any static route for the same CIDR.
glossary.Blackhole route: A static route with no valid attachment target. Any packet whose destination matches a blackhole route is silently dropped by the Transit Gateway with no ICMP unreachable returned to the source.
glossary.Static-vs-propagated priority rule: When a TGW route table contains both a static route and a propagated route for the same CIDR, the static route always wins regardless of the static route's state. A blackhole static route therefore silently outranks an active propagated route.
glossary.Reachability Analyzer: An AWS VPC feature that performs logical path analysis between two endpoints without sending live traffic. Returns a pass or fail result and, on failure, an explanation code such as TGW_RTB_HIGHER_PRIORITY_ROUTE that pinpoints the blocking component.
glossary.PacketDropCountBlackhole: A CloudWatch metric in the AWS/TransitGateway namespace that counts packets dropped due to blackhole routes. Can be filtered by the TransitGatewayAttachment dimension to identify which attachment's traffic is being dropped.

system.what_broke: A static route for 10.19.0.0/16 with State: blackhole in TGW route table tgw-rtb-main was created six months ago and has higher priority than the propagated route from the payroll VPC attachment. All east-west traffic destined for the payroll VPC is silently dropped at the Transit Gateway. The outage was discovered when an engineer ran a Reachability Analyzer path check while configuring routing for the newly attached VPC and received a TGW_RTB_HIGHER_PRIORITY_ROUTE explanation.

components.name: msb-tgw-01 (Transit Gateway)
components.role: Network hub for all 14 production VPCs at Marsh Street Bank. Route table tgw-rtb-main is associated with every VPC attachment. All inter-VPC traffic flows through this TGW.
connections: tgw-rtb-main associated with all VPC attachments
Forwards packets between payroll-vpc, shared-services-vpc, and 12 other VPCs
components.failure_impact: Route table misconfiguration silently drops inter-VPC traffic without ICMP unreachable; source applications see connection timeouts with no routing error.

components.name: tgw-rtb-main (TGW Route Table)
components.role: Single route table associated with all VPC attachments at Marsh Street Bank. Contains two conflicting route entries for 10.19.0.0/16: one static blackhole and one propagated active route.
components.failure_impact: The static blackhole entry shadows the propagated route from tgw-attach-payroll. All traffic whose destination falls in 10.19.0.0/16 is dropped silently at the TGW.

components.name: payroll-vpc (10.19.0.0/16, vpc-payroll)
components.role: Hosts payroll processing workloads that require east-west access to shared-services-vpc for Active Directory authentication, DNS, and monitoring agents.
connections: Depends on shared-services-vpc for AD, DNS, and monitoring
components.failure_impact: All inter-VPC connections from other VPCs to payroll-vpc fail while the blackhole is active. VPC flow logs on payroll ENIs show no inbound traffic; source ENIs show ACCEPT on egress with no return traffic.

components.name: shared-services-vpc (10.0.0.0/16, vpc-shared)
components.role: Internal shared services hub providing Active Directory, DNS, and monitoring infrastructure consumed by all other VPCs including payroll-vpc.
connections: Serves payroll-vpc for AD auth, DNS resolution, and monitoring agents
components.failure_impact: Payroll authentication and name resolution fail silently. Symptoms appear at the application layer as timeouts and auth errors, not obviously linked to TGW routing.

components.name: new-vpc (10.42.0.0/16, vpc-new, attachment tgw-attach-new)
components.role: New VPC attached to msb-tgw-01 one hour before incident discovery. An engineer ran Reachability Analyzer during its routing setup to verify end-to-end paths; the path incidentally crossed the 10.19.0.0/16 CIDR and exposed the pre-existing blackhole.
connections: Attached to msb-tgw-01 via tgw-attach-new one hour before incident
Reachability Analyzer path analysis triggered the discovery of the blackhole
components.failure_impact: The new VPC itself is unaffected; its attachment was the catalyst that surfaced the six-month-old blackhole during routine routing verification.

progressive_clues: Payroll VPC cannot reach shared services. The Transit Gateway is in the path. Check the route table.
The TGW route table has two routes for 10.19.0.0/16: one static, one propagated. Static routes always win over propagated routes for the same CIDR.
The static route for 10.19.0.0/16 is in BLACKHOLE state. A blackhole route has no valid attachment; matching packets are dropped silently. It still outranks the propagated route.
Call DeleteTransitGatewayRoute on the blackhole static entry for 10.19.0.0/16 in tgw-rtb-main. The propagated route from tgw-attach-payroll will become the active path.

resolution.root_cause: A static route for 10.19.0.0/16 with State: blackhole in TGW route table tgw-rtb-main has higher priority than the propagated route from the payroll VPC attachment, causing all east-west traffic destined for the payroll VPC to be silently dropped at the Transit Gateway.

fix_criteria.description: Player identifies the static blackhole route for 10.19.0.0/16 in tgw-rtb-main from the TGW route table
Player explains that a static route takes priority over a propagated route for the same CIDR regardless of state
Player calls DeleteTransitGatewayRoute for the 10.19.0.0/16 blackhole entry, restoring the propagated route as active
Player proposes a CloudWatch alarm on PacketDropCountBlackhole or an AWS Config rule to detect blackhole routes before they cause outages

learning_objectives: Static routes in a TGW route table outrank propagated routes for the same CIDR even when the static route is in BLACKHOLE state.
A blackhole route silently drops packets; VPC flow logs on the source ENI show ACCEPT on egress but no return-path entries, because the drop occurs at the TGW after leaving the source VPC.
PacketDropCountBlackhole in the AWS/TransitGateway namespace is the canonical metric for blackhole-route drops; Reachability Analyzer returns TGW_RTB_HIGHER_PRIORITY_ROUTE for the same condition.
sop_steps: Check CloudWatch metric PacketDropCountBlackhole for the TGW and per attachment using the TransitGatewayAttachment dimension. Non-zero values confirm blackhole-route drops are occurring.
Run SearchTransitGatewayRoutes on the TGW route table with filter State=blackhole. Any result is an active problem that must be investigated.
For each blackhole result, check whether a propagated route for the same CIDR exists. If yes, the blackhole silently shadows it and all traffic to that CIDR is being dropped.
Call DeleteTransitGatewayRoute specifying TransitGatewayRouteTableId and DestinationCidrBlock of the blackhole entry to remove the shadowing route.
Verify with Reachability Analyzer: run StartNetworkInsightsAnalysis on a path from the payroll-VPC ENI to a shared-services-VPC ENI. Expect networkPathFound: true after the fix.

Prevent recurrence: create a CloudWatch alarm on PacketDropCountBlackhole at threshold 0 per attachment. Optionally add an AWS Config rule that flags TGW route tables containing any blackhole-state static routes.

related_failure_modes.scenario: Missing propagated route: VPC attachment exists but route propagation is not enabled on the route table association, so no route exists at all for the CIDR.
related_failure_modes.how_it_differs: Reachability Analyzer returns TGW_RTB_NO_ROUTE_TO_TGW_ATTACHMENT; SearchTransitGatewayRoutes returns no route for the CIDR rather than a blackhole entry. There is no static-vs-propagated conflict; the route table simply has no entry.
related_failure_modes.prevention: Enforce route propagation on all attachments via AWS Config rule. Alert when a new attachment has no propagated route within 5 minutes of creation.

related_failure_modes.scenario: Overlapping CIDR conflict: a more-specific static route (for example 10.19.1.0/24) coexists with a propagated /16, causing partial reachability that varies by destination IP.
related_failure_modes.how_it_differs: Some payroll hosts are reachable and some are not depending on whether their IP falls in the more-specific prefix. Reachability Analyzer returns TGW_RTB_MORE_SPECIFIC_ROUTE; no blackhole flag appears in the route state.
related_failure_modes.prevention: Audit for overlapping CIDRs before adding any static route. Run SearchTransitGatewayRoutes after every route-table change to compare static and propagated entries and confirm no unintended overlap.

sop_practices: Run SearchTransitGatewayRoutes with filter State=blackhole after every TGW route-table change as a mandatory post-change health check. A blackhole that was always present but suddenly matters because workloads shifted will surface before an incident is reported.

Alarm on PacketDropCountBlackhole per attachment at threshold 0. A blackhole created during a maintenance window six months ago will be caught the moment traffic begins flowing through the affected path.
